128.source: Tom stood outside the arena, his palms sweaty and heart racing. The crowd's roar was deafening, echoing through every corner. He took a deep breath, knowing that the next few moments were critical. He wasn't sure if his guts could handle the pressure of the situation. He really hadn't been feeling well lately and thought they could literally explode.

704.target: toward a lower or inferior state
704.source: Jack had always loved the thrill of mountain biking. Every weekend, he packed his gear and headed to the trails. He felt alive and unstoppable once he left the familiar comfort of home. He started going downhill after leaving home. He had overtaxed himself and felt winded; Jack felt stupid for not noticing the signs sooner.

736.source: The kids decided to build a soapbox car. They worked hard all afternoon, excited to race it on the steep hill by their street. As they lined it up at the top, they laughed and cheered in anticipation. They didn't realize how fast it would all go downhill. Some of the nails were falling out already as they pushed it to the start; they weren't sure it would make it through the journey.

1408.target: deprive someone of benefits, as a penalty
1408.source: Sean had always dreamt of working by the sea. His job at the harbor kept him busy, overseeing shipments and coordinating arrivals. Yesterday, a hiccup in the schedule threw everything into chaos. Sean was docked pay as soon as he arrived late yesterday. He was annoyed by this removal of income.

2016.target: the ability to see; the visual faculty
2016.source: Alex woke up to a bright light flooding his room. He rubbed his eyes, trying to make sense of the strange shapes and colors. The images danced before him, indistinct and puzzling. Alex found it hard to clear his vision. He went to the bathroom and poured water over his eyes, and lo and behold, he was able to see clearly.

2048.source: John sat silently in the dimly lit room. He had been pondering the mysterious events of the past week. Each night, vivid details filled his mind as he tried to make sense of it all. 2080.source: Harry loved working in the garden, his hands always busy among the flowers. Over the years, he had developed a reputation for having an eye for the best blooms. However, his friends sometimes joked about how picky he could be with everything in life. Harry had a bad tendency to pick without discernment or discrimination. He just chooses what he wants and thinks about it later.

2176.source: Lily was sitting by the pond, listening to the gentle rustling of the leaves. She had been visiting this spot every day, hoping to see some wildlife. A frog appeared on a rock, looking still and mysterious. The frog began to croak. It was a warning of what was to come next: a storm where the wildlife died.

2208.source: The boy returned home from school on a stormy day. He found his father sitting quietly in the dimly lit living room. Somehow, he seemed different as he stared into nothingness. The boy thought his father had croaked. He rushed and grabbed him, but he was just deep in thought.

2304.target: (of meat) cooked a short time; still red inside
2304.source: James walked into the old butcher shop, eyeing the selection carefully. He remembered the legend of a steak so unique it could only be found once in a blue moon. The shopkeeper smiled, sensing James's curiosity as he pointed out a special cut. Well, this particular steak has to be rare. James understood then that this wasn't just about how the steak was cooked, but about the exceptional quality that made it a truly infrequent find.

2400.target: an itemized statement of money owed for goods shipped or services rendered
2400.source: Emma sat at the quaint corner cafe, enjoying her afternoon tea. She absentmindedly watched as a few birds fluttered onto the nearby tables. A waiter approached her table, ready to deliver a piece of paper. The bill made contact with the table in a quick manner. She paid it as quickly as she could.

2432.source: Lily had stayed up late the night before, going over her notes one last time. She knew how important this exam was for her future. Her alarm was set for early in the morning to give her plenty of time to prepare. By 6 a.m., she was already up and ready for the big exam. She started her day with eggs, bacon, and toast while drying her shirt by the heater.

2496.target: a brief version of the essential meaning of something
2496.source: Tom was deep in thought as he faced the cluttered shelf. On it, there lay a map rolled up beside an old hunting knife. He knew he had to make his decision now. He got to the point. Unfurling the map, he traced a finger along the coastline until he found what he was looking for. "There! That's where we'll go fishing." And with that, he pocketed the knife and ran out the door, down to the boatshed where everyone was waiting.

2624.source: Tom was eager to blend in at the comedy show. He settled into one of the middle rows of the theater, trying not to draw attention. Every morning, he liked to glance out of his window to see what was happening outside. He could see a plant from his window. He had watched the magic show in the hotel garden many times and knew that the man in the audience was in cahoots with the magician.

2912.source: The siblings spent their summer exploring the beach. They often searched for interesting things that washed ashore. One day, they stumbled upon something curious hidden in the sand.
